items.title: Electrified Weekly – launch deals on ENGWE M20 3.0 moped e-bike + Hiboy EX11 e-bike, EcoFlow and Anker flash sales, more
items.summary:
Welcome to 9to5Toys Electrified Weekly – the best place to find all of this week’s best deals and new releases to electrify your life and protect Mother Nature. It’s been an exciting week of Green Deals, with this week’s roundup primarily focused on notable e-bike releases and power station deals/sales – though we also have quite the collection of tool deals, too. The two big EV deals were the launch savings on the ENGWE M20 3.0 Moped-Style e-bikes that feature two battery configurations for up to 180 miles of travel, starting from $1,399, as well as Hiboy’s EX11 Full Suspension e-bike that boasts up to 100 miles of travel at $1,300. We also secured an exclusive $500 discount on the Jackery Explorer 1500 v2 Power Station at a new $499 low, while EcoFlow and Anker SOLIX have launched weekend flash sales that are offering some very big savings – like the expanded SOLIX F3000 6,144Wh bundle back at a $1,999 low. All these and more are waiting for you below!

items.title: Save up to $30 while upgrading your home theater with these Govee TV Backlight 3 Lite sets starting from $55
items.summary:
If you’ve been wanting to upgrade your home theater system with reactive smart lighting, Govee’s official Amazon storefront is offering the TV Backlight 3 Lite Kit at $119.99 shipped, which is not available directly from the brand’s website. This kit would fetch $150 at full price, with it having been keeping down at this rate since mid-January. While we’ve seen it go a bit lower in the past, you’re still looking at a solid $30 markdown here that lets you upgrade your media viewing experience at the second-best price of 2026 and the third-best overall. If you want to get the same reactive backlighting for your TVs of 40 to 85 inches in size while saving a bit more without the smart light bars in the above package, you can find Govee’s three standard TV Backlight 3 Lite sizes starting from $54.99 shipped right now.

One notable deal is on the LEGO Super Mario World – Mario & Yoshi set at a $103.99 shipped. Normally $130 at full price, which is what you’d be paying getting it direct from LEGO’s website or stores, this set has been holding out at this rate since mid-February, with the previous time we saw it drop this low being in September 2025. While this deal lasts, which is likely until after Mario Day (March 10), you can score $26 off the going rate for the lowest price we have tracked in its history. You’ll find all the discounted sets rounded up below, as well as some of our favorite picks that are sadly not getting any discounts.
